does: This paper introduces Trusted Contact, a safety feature integrated into ChatGPT that detects conversations suggesting serious self-harm risk and automatically notifies a user-designated trusted person. The system allows users to proactively identify a contact who receives alerts when the AI identifies concerning patterns in their conversations. The feature operates with the user's explicit consent and aims to bridge the gap between AI detection capabilities and real-world intervention by connecting digital safety signals to trusted human networks who can provide immediate support.
connects: This work builds on a growing body of research in AI-assisted mental health monitoring and crisis intervention systems. It extends prior work on detecting suicide risk and self-harm indicators in text through machine learning, drawing on psychological literature about warning signs and clinical risk assessment. The approach also connects to research on human-AI collaboration in safety-critical domains and the broader ecosystem of digital mental health interventions, including crisis chatbots and helpline systems that have attempted to identify and route high-risk individuals to appropriate care.
enables: This work opens pathways for broader integration of AI safety systems with real-world support networks and formal mental health infrastructure. It demonstrates a model where AI detection feeds into human intervention loops rather than attempting autonomous crisis response. Future developments could extend this approach to other high-risk conditions, integrate with emergency services protocols, validate detection accuracy across diverse populations, and explore privacy-preserving notification mechanisms that respect user autonomy while maximizing intervention effectiveness in critical moments.
